name: GALLSTONE ILEUS
definition: gastric outlet obstruction ← impacted gallstone in duodenum / pylorus ← cholecystoenteric

demographics.Frequency: 0.4-5.0% of all intestinal obstructions ← risk increases with age = 20% [24%] of
demographics.Etiology: biliary disease (90%), peptic ulcer disease, cancer, trauma

dominant_finding: obstructing ectopic gallstone in distal stomach / duodenum
discriminating_features: Rigler triad on plain film (in 15%), on CT (in 78%):
change in position of previously identified gallstone
smooth polypoid filling defect (= gallstone) at site of obstruction
well-contained localized barium collection lateral to first portion of duodenum (barium-
air / enteric contrast in GB (fistula from GB to duodenum)

general: Rigler triad on plain film (in 15%), on CT (in 78%):
change in position of previously identified gallstone
smooth polypoid filling defect (= gallstone) at site of obstruction
well-contained localized barium collection lateral to first portion of duodenum (barium-
air / enteric contrast in GB (fistula from GB to duodenum)
gallstone at transition point of bowel obstruction
CT: obstructing ectopic gallstone in distal stomach / duodenum

clinical.Cx: recurrent gallstone ileus in 5-10% (additional silent calculi more proximally)
clinical.Rx: removal of stone(s) + cholecystectomy + fistula repair
